title: EDL — the per-ad edit plan (v1)
description: The single artifact the planner (editor LLM) and the renderer (code) share. The planner never knows how render.py works; render.py never knows why the plan is what it is. Validated by tools/edl_lint.py before any render.

style.description: Which style from knowledge/styles/ this edit fits (e.g. 'split', 'cutaway'). The style's flex params shaped the defaults.

output.description: Delivery format. Default 1080×1920@30 (vertical social). render.py reframes any source to this aspect.

sources.description: clip_id → absolute local path of the cached source clip.

ranges.description: The talking-head spine, in output order. Each range is a word-boundary-snapped, padded cut.

punch_in.description: Scale factor within the source's punchin_max. 1.0 = none.

broll.description: B-roll placements. Each carries its own evidence so edl_lint (B8) can verify claim↔tag.

moment_start.description: In-point in the B-roll source clip.

claim.description: The spoken claim this B-roll answers, e.g. 'your dog barks at the door'.

moment_tags.description: Taxonomy tags of the chosen moment window. B8 checks these confirm the claim.

overlays.description: Rendered animation clips (from parallel animation sub-agents).

captions.description: Caption styling; text + timings come from the cached transcript at render time.

end_card.description: Our-offer CTA (RULES D4). Geometry inside the safe area (B9).
